steps: A line of symmetry divides a shape into two identical halves that are mirror images. For a square: 1) Two lines through the midpoints of opposite sides (horizontal and vertical). 2) Two lines through opposite corners (diagonals). That gives 4 lines of symmetry. Checking options: A) 1 is too few. B) 2 is only the horizontal and vertical, ignoring diagonals. C) 3 is not correct. D) 4 matches. E) 8 is too many (a square does not have 8 lines of symmetry).
